inline.body:
<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Direct violation of CONVENTIONS.md by deleting shared copyDir helper — CONVENTIONS.md explicitly states that recursive/directory copies must go through the project's `copyDir` helper and that removing or swapping it for `fs.cpSync` is a regression. This PR deletes the helper entirely at this location, violating the documented convention.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Direct violation of CONVENTIONS.md — fs.cpSync called instead of copyDir — The PR replaces the copyDir call with a direct `fs.cpSync(publicDir, outDir, …)` call, violating the CONVENTIONS.md rule that all directory copies must go through the copyDir helper.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Direct violation of CONVENTIONS.md — fs.cpSync called instead of copyDir in scaffolding — The PR replaces copyDir with a direct `fs.cpSync` call in the template-scaffolding path and deletes the local copy/copyDir, violating CONVENTIONS.md.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Symlink behavior silently changed — dereference: false breaks publicDir copies — The old `copyDir` used `fs.statSync` (which follows symlinks) and then `copyFileSync`, resolving symlink content into the destination. The new `fs.cpSync` call passes `{ recursive: true, mode: COPYFILE_FICLONE }` without `dereference: true`, so symlinks in publicDir are copied as dangling symlinks into outDir, breaking served assets (404 / broken asset). At minimum `dereference: true` must be passed.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Symlink behavior silently changed in scaffolding — dereference: false — Same symlink regression as in prepareOutDir: a symlinked file in a template would become a symlink in the scaffolded project rather than its resolved content, because `dereference: true` is not passed to `fs.cpSync`.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Removed self-recursion guard for nested outDir/publicDir configurations — The deleted `copyDir` had a deliberate guard (`if (srcFile === destDir) continue`) that silently skipped entries where an entry inside srcDir resolved to destDir itself (e.g., outDir under publicDir). `fs.cpSync` throws `ERR_FS_CP_INVALID_ARGUMENT` in that case instead of silently proceeding, an observable behavior change for monorepo setups.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — fs.cpSync emits ExperimentalWarning on Node <22.3; eslint-disable hides the issue — An `eslint-disable-next-line n/no-unsupported-features/node-builtins` comment suppresses the linter warning, but does not fix the runtime behavior. On Node 16.7–22.2 (including current Node 20.x LTS), `fs.cpSync` prints an `ExperimentalWarning` to stderr on every call, appearing during every `vite build` that copies publicDir. The declared minimum Node version must be confirmed before landing.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — fs.cpSync emits ExperimentalWarning on Node <22.3 shown to every scaffolding user — Same issue as in prepareOutDir: on Node 20.x LTS, the `ExperimentalWarning` is shown to every user scaffolding a project via create-vite. The eslint-disable silences the linter but not the runtime warning.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Unrelated behavior change: COPYFILE_FICLONE mode added — Both call sites add `mode: fs.constants.COPYFILE_FICLONE`, requesting copy-on-write reflinks where supported. The old `copyFileSync` used the default mode (plain content copy). This is a behavior change unrelated to the stated PR intent ('use fs.cpSync instead of custom copyDir') — scope creep.

sticky:
<!-- squarewright:review -->

## Squarewright review

The PR replaces a custom `copyDir` helper with `fs.cpSync` in three locations, directly contradicting a documented project convention and introducing multiple behavioral regressions: broken symlink dereference, removal of a self-recursion guard, ExperimentalWarning on supported Node versions, and an unrelated COPYFILE_FICLONE mode change. Recommended rejection as written.

**9** finding(s): 🔴 5  ·  🟡 3  ·  🔵 1

- 🔴 `packages/vite/src/node/utils.ts:627` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Direct violation of CONVENTIONS.md by deleting shared copyDir helper — CONVENTIONS.md explicitly states that recursive/directory copies must go through the project's `copyDir` helper and that removing or swapping it for `fs.cpSync` is a regression. This PR deletes the helper entirely at this location, violating the documented convention.

  ```suggestion
  null
  ```
- 🔴 `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:89` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Direct violation of CONVENTIONS.md — fs.cpSync called instead of copyDir — The PR replaces the copyDir call with a direct `fs.cpSync(publicDir, outDir, …)` call, violating the CONVENTIONS.md rule that all directory copies must go through the copyDir helper.

  ```suggestion
  null
  ```
- 🔴 `packages/create-vite/src/index.ts:647` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Direct violation of CONVENTIONS.md — fs.cpSync called instead of copyDir in scaffolding — The PR replaces copyDir with a direct `fs.cpSync` call in the template-scaffolding path and deletes the local copy/copyDir, violating CONVENTIONS.md.

  ```suggestion
  null
  ```
- 🔴 `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:89` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Symlink behavior silently changed — dereference: false breaks publicDir copies — The old `copyDir` used `fs.statSync` (which follows symlinks) and then `copyFileSync`, resolving symlink content into the destination. The new `fs.cpSync` call passes `{ recursive: true, mode: COPYFILE_FICLONE }` without `dereference: true`, so symlinks in publicDir are copied as dangling symlinks into outDir, breaking served assets (404 / broken asset). At minimum `dereference: true` must be passed.

  ```suggestion
  fs.cpSync(src, dest, { recursive: true, dereference: true, mode: fs.constants.COPYFILE_FICLONE })
  ```
- 🔴 `packages/create-vite/src/index.ts:647` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Symlink behavior silently changed in scaffolding — dereference: false — Same symlink regression as in prepareOutDir: a symlinked file in a template would become a symlink in the scaffolded project rather than its resolved content, because `dereference: true` is not passed to `fs.cpSync`.

  ```suggestion
  fs.cpSync(src, dest, { recursive: true, dereference: true, mode: fs.constants.COPYFILE_FICLONE })
  ```
- 🟡 `packages/vite/src/node/utils.ts:627` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Removed self-recursion guard for nested outDir/publicDir configurations — The deleted `copyDir` had a deliberate guard (`if (srcFile === destDir) continue`) that silently skipped entries where an entry inside srcDir resolved to destDir itself (e.g., outDir under publicDir). `fs.cpSync` throws `ERR_FS_CP_INVALID_ARGUMENT` in that case instead of silently proceeding, an observable behavior change for monorepo setups.

  ```suggestion
  null
  ```
- 🟡 `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:88` _[Correctness, Security]_ — fs.cpSync emits ExperimentalWarning on Node <22.3; eslint-disable hides the issue — An `eslint-disable-next-line n/no-unsupported-features/node-builtins` comment suppresses the linter warning, but does not fix the runtime behavior. On Node 16.7–22.2 (including current Node 20.x LTS), `fs.cpSync` prints an `ExperimentalWarning` to stderr on every call, appearing during every `vite build` that copies publicDir. The declared minimum Node version must be confirmed before landing.

  ```suggestion
  null
  ```
- 🟡 `packages/create-vite/src/index.ts:646` _[Correctness, Security]_ — fs.cpSync emits ExperimentalWarning on Node <22.3 shown to every scaffolding user — Same issue as in prepareOutDir: on Node 20.x LTS, the `ExperimentalWarning` is shown to every user scaffolding a project via create-vite. The eslint-disable silences the linter but not the runtime warning.

  ```suggestion
  null
  ```
- 🔵 `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:88` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Unrelated behavior change: COPYFILE_FICLONE mode added — Both call sites add `mode: fs.constants.COPYFILE_FICLONE`, requesting copy-on-write reflinks where supported. The old `copyFileSync` used the default mode (plain content copy). This is a behavior change unrelated to the stated PR intent ('use fs.cpSync instead of custom copyDir') — scope creep.

  ```suggestion
  null
  ```
